channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Pathogenesis & Basic Science
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Pathogenesis & Basic Science

item.title: Revisiting pharmacological actions of disodium cromoglycate in light of GPR35: New therapeutic contexts for classical drugs.
item.description: Biochemical pharmacology - Review - Mast cell stabilizers such as disodium cromoglycate have been used for decades as prophylactic anti-allergic agents, yet their molecular targets remained unknown, and their intracellular mechanisms...

item.title: Discovery of an oral, potent and highly selective immunoproteasome inhibitor for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis and ulcerative colitis.
item.description: European journal of medicinal chemistry - The immunoproteasome plays a critical role in enhancing antigen presentation, regulating inflammatory responses and modulating T-cell differentiation. Its overexpression can lead to abnormal genera...

item.title: Ameliorative Effects of Newly Developed Citrus Hybrid "Mubong" Peel Extract on Experimental Colitis and Gut Microbiota Dysbiosis.
item.description: Food science & nutrition - "Mubong," a newly developed citrus hybrid, is recognized for its unique flavor profile, yet its chemical composition and therapeutic potential remain unexplored. In the present work, we profiled th...

item.title: Orientin alleviates ulcerative colitis by regulating ferroptosis and glycolysis via the ROS/HIF-1α/GLUT1 signaling axis.
item.description: Journal of ethnopharmacology - Crataegus pinnatifida Bunge, a plant used traditionally in herbal medicine for digestive health, is known for its gut-protective properties. Orientin, a natural flavonoid compound abundant in this ...

item.title: Natural chlorophyll‑sodium alginate oral hydrogel for robust treatment of ulcerative colitis.
item.description: International journal of biological macromolecules - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic and relapsing inflammatory bowel disorder that may lead to debilitating symptoms and serious complications such as toxic megacolon, intestinal perforation, and ...

item.title: Preparation, characterization, and preventive effects of ginseng polysaccharide-stabilized selenium nanoparticles against DSS-induced colitis.
item.description: Nanomedicine (London, England) - To overcome the poor colloidal stability of selenium nanoparticles (SeNPs) and combine ginseng polysaccharide bioactivity with nano selenium advantages for ulcerative colitis prevention. Ginseng po...

item.title: T cell transfer into immunodeficient mice uncovers a paralytic phenotype beyond colitis.
item.description: Experimental animals - Adoptive transfer of CD45RBhigh T cells into immunodeficient mice is a well-established model for inducing colitis. Here, we report for the first time that a subset of these mice unexpectedly devel...

item.title: Lactobacilli Mitigate Gut Inflammation by Modulating T-Helper/T-Regulatory Cells and Suppressing Signaling Pathways in Food-Derived Models.
item.description: Probiotics and antimicrobial proteins - Review - Intestinal inflammation disrupts epithelial integrity, fuels antibiotic resistance, and underlies disorders such as inflammatory bowel disease. Lactobacillus species, established inhabitants of tra...

item.title: Ginseng marc-derived low-molecular-weight neutral glucan SN-GOS ameliorates ulcerative colitis via inhibition of apoptosis and activation of autophagy in human intestinal HT-29 cells and DSS-induced inflammatory colitis mice.
item.description: Carbohydrate research - Although ginseng marc has been routinely discarded as a waste, it still contains considerable amounts of bioactive compounds, including polysaccharides. This study assessed the protective effects o...

item.title: Circulating PEG-indoleamine 2,3-dioxygenase ameliorates diverse inflammatory diseases without toxicity or compromising immunocompetence.
item.description: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America - Indoleamine 2,3-dioxygenase (IDO), the enzyme responsible for tryptophan catabolism, is protective in many autoimmune and inflammatory diseases. We previously developed a localized immunomodulation...

item.title: Harmaline and the gut-brain-immune axis: a novel therapeutic avenue in neuroinflammation and ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Inflammopharmacology - Review - Chronic inflammatory disorders like neuroinflammatory disorders and ulcerative colitis (UC) have some common pathogenic mechanisms such as persistent activation of nuclear factor-κB (NF-κB), oxidat...

item.title: Computational Toxicology Prioritization of CYP3A4 and DPP7 as Candidate Triclosan-Relevant Molecular Targets in Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Toxicology mechanisms and methods - Triclosan (TCS), a synthetic broad-spectrum antimicrobial classified as a novel persistent organic pollutant, is detected in over 75% of human urine samples, yet its molecular targets in ulcerative...

item.title: SLFN11 binds DDX1 to counteract human mesenchymal stem/stromal cell senescence through inhibition of mTOR/eIF4E signaling pathway activation.
item.description: Life sciences - Mesenchymal stem/stromal cells (MSCs) are indispensable for cellular therapy; however, long-term in vitro expansion triggers replicative and stress-induced senescence, which undermines their stemne...

item.title: Preventive Effects of Crataegus monogyna Fruit Extract on Oxidative Damage and Inflammatory Response in a Rat Model of Acetic Acid-Induced Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Food science & nutrition - BackgroundUlcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease associated with oxidative stress and inflammation. Natural products may offer protective effects, though their mechanisms r...

item.title: Exosomes from IL-33-Stimulated Macrophages Regulate Epithelial Barrier Function to Ameliorate TNBS-Induced Colitis in Mice.
item.description: Cells - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) represents a growing global health threat that markedly increases colorectal cancer risk, yet conventional immunosuppressive agents achieve mucosal healing in only ...

item.title: Procaine hydrochloride improves DSS-induced colitis via inhibition of IL-6/STAT3 and FOXP3 methylation.
item.description: European cytokine network - Disruption of immune barrier function is considered a hallmark feature of inflammatory bowel disease. In the present study, the potential effects of the methyltransferase inhibitor procaine hydroch...

item.title: GM-CSF promotes pro-inflammatory macrophage activation associated with Akt/mTOR signaling during experimental colitis.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is an intestinal immune disorder of unknown etiology. Mounting evidence reveals a central role of macrophages in the hemostatic balance of gut immunity, and dysfunctional ma...

item.title: Holarrhena pubescens (Kutaj) bark extract mitigates SDS-induced IBD-like pathologies in Drosophila melanogaster by preserving barrier integrity and antioxidant defense.
item.description: Frontiers in pharmacology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a group of chronic conditions characterized by relapsing inflammation of gastrointestinal tract. Conventional treatments for IBD are confronted with various chal...

item.title: Five-flavor Sophora flavescens Enteric-coated Capsules Alleviate Experimental Colitis and Ferroptosis-related Changes.
item.description: Journal of visualized experiments : JoVE - Ulcerative colitis remains difficult to manage because effective and targeted therapeutic options are limited, and oxidative stress, inflammatory signaling, and regulated cell-death pathways may co...
